# RESOURCES.md Format

Curated trusted sources. Knowledge comes from here, not parametric guesses. Wisdom comes from the communities listed here.

## Structure

```md
# {Topic} Resources

## Knowledge
- [Book: _Title_ — Author](https://example.com)
  What it covers. Use for: {when to reach for it}.
- [Article: "Title" — Author (Site)](https://example.com)
  What it covers. Use for: {when to reach for it}.

## Wisdom (Communities)
- [r/subreddit](https://reddit.com/r/...)
  High-signal, moderated. Use for: {plateau troubleshooting, critique}.
- Local: {class/group name}
  Use for: real-time feedback.
```

## Rules

- High-trust only. Primary sources, recognised experts, peer-reviewed work, well-moderated communities. Drop marketing-as-education.
- Annotate every entry — one line: what it covers + when to use. Bare links are useless in 3 months.
- Group by Knowledge / Wisdom. A resource can sit in just one.
- Surface gaps. No good source for a needed area? Add a `## Gaps` section. It drives future search.
- Prune ruthlessly. Five sharp sources beat thirty mediocre ones.
- Record community opt-out so future sessions stop proposing them.
